headlines.title: Colombia's Cocaine Trade Now Outearns Its Oil Exports
headlines.summary: Drug lords in Colombia are making more money from cocaine than the government is making from crude oil sales, a report from a Colombian university has found. At $16.5 billion for 2024, cocaine revenues surpassed oil export revenues, which stood at around $15 billion that year, UP

headlines.title: SpaceX Wants to Fuel Its Mars Ambitions With Its Own Gas Pipeline
headlines.summary: SpaceX has spent years trying to reinvent rockets. Now it's coming after midstream. The aerospace giant plans to begin construction next month on an eight-mile natural gas pipeline dubbed "Starpipe" that will feed its Starbase launch facility in South Texas, according to filings

headlines.title: Kazakhstan Lands $12 Billion in EU Deals to Expand Energy and Trade Ties
headlines.summary: Kazakh President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev’s visit to the EU’s headquarters in Brussels has yielded agreements and MoUs potentially valued at over 12 billion dollars. headlines.summary: Russia has emerged as one of the clearest commercial beneficiaries of the US-Israel war with Iran. headlines.title: Major Saudi Oil Terminal Roars Back to Life as Gulf Supply Recovery Accelerates
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia's biggest oil export terminal is finally stirring back to life, and that's a much bigger deal than another headline about the Strait of Hormuz reopening. headlines.title: UK Energy Secretary Vetoed Plan to Boost Oil Output for Defense Funds
headlines.summary: UK Energy Secretary Ed Miliband has vetoed a plan by the Treasury to boost North Sea oil and gas drilling in order to fund part of Britain’s increased defense funding needs, The Telegraph reported on Thursday, citing a government source.
